codeReview.summary: The commit improves low-confidence filtering and fuzzy duplicate suppression in the capture path, but it still does not satisfy the feature's required claweb cross-turn proof and its confidence heuristic is overly strict for plain durable preference statements.

issues.description: After a message matches a durable capture category, muninnAutoCaptureConfidence still rejects plain durable preferences unless they include booster phrases such as `every project`, `from now on`, or similar. For example, `I prefer dark mode for this repo.` or `My preferred editor is Helix.` reaches the preference branch but scores only 0.86 because the base 0.78 plus the `prefer` or `preferred` bonus 0.08 never reaches the 0.90 threshold, so durable preferences are dropped even though the feature is supposed to filter low-confidence noise, not require special phrasing.

The added end-to-end test only verifies that a duplicate write is skipped during a single ProcessDirectWithChannel turn. It does not add any claweb multi-turn test or live evidence that a fact captured in one claweb turn is automatically recalled in a later turn without manual memory tools, so VAL-CROSS-001 remains unproven in this feature review.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The go-memory-worker skill requires manual claweb verification for end-to-end or cross-turn behavior, but this worker handoff substituted code-path review for that required live check.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: D:\tmp\claw\picoclaw\.factory\skills\go-memory-worker\SKILL.md step 7 requires manual verification for cross-turn flow; handoff C:\Users\杨旭\.factory\missions\2f567679-e314-4c0f-83af-a6c10ee2820a\handoffs\2026-03-19T09-42-23-426Z__muninn-capture-dedupe-and-claweb-cross-turn-flow__1cc2fd87-1f42-4b31-abf3-278ce1cb9341.json reports only tests/lint plus an interactiveChecks note that it merely reviewed the capture path and test-backed request flow.

summary: Fail: the dedupe improvements are plausible, but the feature still lacks the required claweb multi-turn capture-then-recall proof and the new confidence gate drops plainly stated durable preferences.
